engineIntelligence.h2: Chrysler 300 / 300C Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Chrysler 300 / 300C in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components to address known weaknesses.

cards.altText: Chrysler 300 / 300C EXL engine - diesel 3.0L replacement UK
knownFailures: Timing chain stretch - causes catastrophic rattling and potential engine failure around 80k-100k miles.
EGR and DPF blockages - leads to limp mode and excessive carbon buildup in the intake.

cards.altText: Chrysler 300 / 300C EZB engine - petrol 5.7L replacement UK
knownFailures: Lifter tick and camshaft phaser wear - notorious failure causing loud tapping and loss of power.
Piston ring wear - leads to high oil consumption and blue exhaust smoke on higher-mileage units.

cards.altText: Chrysler 300 / 300C ERB engine - petrol 3.6L replacement UK
knownFailures: Oil cooler leak - causes oil and coolant to mix, frequently leading to subsequent head gasket failure.
Timing chain tensioner wear - can cause rattling on cold starts in post-2011 models.

engineGuide.h2: Chrysler 300 / 300C Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Chrysler 300 / 300C across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 3.0L Diesel
entries.history: The backbone of the UK 300C diesel range, offering strong torque for the heavy Touring estate, but notorious for timing chain vulnerabilities.
commonFailures: Timing chain stretch and tensioner failure (80k-100k miles).
EGR valve sticking and DPF catastrophic blockage.
entries.cta: Get quotes for EXL Chrysler 300 / 300C engine replacement

entries.title: 2.7L Diesel
entries.history: The early-generation diesel option, largely phased out due to reliability concerns regarding the intake manifold and swirl flaps.
commonFailures: Swirl flap disintegration causing intake manifold destruction.
Oil cooler seal failures leading to coolant contamination.
entries.cta: Get quotes for ENR Chrysler 300 / 300C engine replacement

entries.title: 5.7L Petrol
entries.history: The iconic American V8, delivering classic HEMI sound and power, but requiring vigilant oil maintenance to prevent valvetrain wear.
commonFailures: Hydraulic lifter tick and camshaft phaser wear.
High oil consumption due to piston ring wear.
entries.cta: Get quotes for EZB Chrysler 300 / 300C engine replacement

entries.title: 6.1L Petrol
entries.history: The ultra-high-performance SRT8 variant, built for speed but requiring specialist knowledge for reliable long-term rebuilds.
commonFailures: Piston skirt cracking under high-stress track or aggressive driving.
Oil pump pickup tube o-ring failure causing oil starvation.
entries.cta: Get quotes for ESF Chrysler 300 / 300C engine replacement

entries.title: 3.6L Petrol
entries.history: Introduced in the facelift model, this smoother, more efficient V6 replaced the older 3.5L unit, though it brought its own oiling quirks.
commonFailures: Oil cooler leak leading to oil/coolant mixing.
Subsequent head gasket failure if overheating occurs.
entries.cta: Get quotes for ERB Chrysler 300 / 300C engine replacement

entries.title: 3.5L Petrol
entries.history: The entry-level petrol unit for the first generation, known for being adequate but prone to overheating if the cooling system is neglected.
commonFailures: Blown head gasket due to cooling system neglect.
Intake manifold gasket leaks causing rough idle.
entries.cta: Get quotes for EGF Chrysler 300 / 300C engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Chrysler 300 / 300C Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Chrysler 300 / 300C is a robust vehicle, but specific engine families dominate replacement demand, notably the 3.0L CRD (EXL) and 5.7L HEMI (EZB). Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use this guide to decide your best path forward.

problems.h4: Chrysler 300 / 300C 3.0 CRD Timing Chain Failure - Why EXL Engines Fail
problems.rootCause: The rear-mounted timing chain and plastic tensioners wear prematurely, stretching the chain and causing it to jump timing or shatter.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Engine removal, new chain, guides, tensioners, and oil pump.
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early before valve/piston contact.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of a fully rebuilt EXL engine with upgraded chain components and 12-month warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: Best long-term solution once catastrophic rattling begins.

problems.recommendation: If you hear a distinct diesel rattling on cold starts, do not delay. A preventative chain kit is cost-effective, but once the chain has jumped, a full rebuilt engine replacement is the only reliable option to protect your investment.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EXL engine prices for your Chrysler 300 / 300C

problems.h4: Chrysler 300 / 300C 5.7 HEMI Lifter Tick - Why EZB Engines Fail
problems.rootCause: Inadequate oil pressure or infrequent oil changes cause hydraulic lifters to collapse, leading to camshaft lobe wear and phaser failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder heads, replace all 16 lifters, pushrods, and damaged camshaft.
repairOptions.longevity: Only recommended if caught at the very first sign of ticking.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of a remanufactured EZB engine with updated valvetrain components and warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: The definitive fix for advanced cam wear or persistent oil consumption.

problems.recommendation: The HEMI "lifter tick" is a well-documented progression. If the tick is accompanied by a check engine light (P0300 misfires), the camshaft is already damaged. At this stage, a rebuilt engine is more economical than a partial top-end repair.
problems.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt 5.7 HEMI EZB engines for your Chrysler 300 / 300C

problems.h4: Chrysler 300 / 300C 2.7 CRD Swirl Flap Failure - Intake Manifold Destruction
problems.rootCause: The plastic swirl flap actuator arms become brittle and snap, dropping debris directly into the cylinders or blocking the intake manifold.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove flaps, install blanking plates, and remap ECU to clear fault codes.
repairOptions.longevity: A preventative measure only. Useless if debris has already entered the engine.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of a rebuilt ENR engine, often with an upgraded metal manifold setup.
repairOptions.longevity: Necessary if the engine has ingested plastic and suffered valve/piston damage.

problems.recommendation: If your 2.7 CRD is running smoothly, invest in a swirl flap delete immediately. If you are already experiencing severe power loss, metallic knocking, or misfires, the engine is likely compromised and requires full replacement.
problems.cta: Check ENR engine replacement options for your Chrysler 300 / 300C

problems.h4: Chrysler 300 / 300C 3.6 Pentastar Oil Cooler Leak - Head Gasket Risk
problems.rootCause: The oil cooler housing gasket degrades, allowing engine oil and coolant to mix, which rapidly leads to overheating and head gasket failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Engine strip, new oil cooler housing, head gasket set, and full cooling system flush.
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if the block and head have not warped from severe overheating.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of a rebuilt ERB engine with updated oil cooler components and 12-month warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: The safest route if the engine has already overheated and warped components.

problems.recommendation: Check your coolant reservoir regularly. If it looks like "chocolate milk", stop driving immediately. A caught early oil cooler swap is affordable, but ignored overheating guarantees a costly rebuilt engine is your only path forward.
problems.cta: Compare 3.6 Pentastar ERB engine prices for your Chrysler 300 / 300C

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Chrysler 300 / 300C
finalCta.paragraph: Navigating engine problems on a Chrysler 300 / 300C can be stressful, but EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK specialists. We connect you directly with vetted rebuilders who supply fully tested, upgraded engines backed by a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty, with nationwide delivery and supply & fit options available. Avoid the main dealer premium and get back on the road with confidence.

Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket’s UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Chrysler 300 / 300C and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineYears.h2: Chrysler 300 / 300C Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: Use this Chrysler 300 300C year guide to match your vehicle to the right engine family, production period, and replacement options.

years.description: The Chrysler 300C arrived in the UK, bringing American V8 muscle and early Mercedes-sourced diesel technology to the executive car market.
keyChanges: Launch of the first-generation Chrysler 300C in the UK market.
Introduction of the 2.7L V6 diesel (ENR) and 3.5L V6 petrol (EGF).
Availability of the iconic 5.7L HEMI V8 (EZB) and the high-performance 6.1L SRT8 (ESF) from 2006.
knownFor: Bold, retro-styling that turned heads on UK roads.
Early 2.7 CRD units suffering from swirl flap and intake manifold failures.
The 5.7 HEMI establishing a strong enthusiast following despite early lifter concerns.
years.cta: Get quotes for 2005-2007 Chrysler 300 / 300C replacement engines
years.ticker: Chrysler 300C 2.7 CRD engine replacement • Chrysler 300C 5.7 HEMI lifter tick fix • Chrysler 300C SRT8 engine rebuild cost

years.description: Chrysler addressed early diesel criticisms by introducing the superior 3.0L CRD engine, which quickly became the dominant UK seller.
keyChanges: Phasing out of the problematic 2.7L CRD in favour of the 3.0L CRD (EXL).
Introduction of the Touring (estate) variant, heavily boosting 3.0L diesel demand.
Refinements to the 5.7L HEMI valvetrain, though lifter tick remained a known risk.
knownFor: The 3.0L CRD becoming the definitive, most-searched engine for replacement.
Timing chain stretch emerging as the primary failure point for the EXL engine.
Strong residual values for well-maintained HEMI V8 examples.
years.ticker: Chrysler 300C 3.0 CRD timing chain replacement • Chrysler 300C Touring engine supply and fit
enquiries: Chrysler 300C Touring engine supply and fit

years.description: A significant generational shift brought the modern, efficient 3.6L Pentastar V6 to replace the aging 3.5L unit, alongside an updated 3.0L CRD.
keyChanges: Complete discontinuation of the 3.5L V6 and 2.7L engines.
Introduction of the 3.6L Pentastar V6 (ERB) as the new standard petrol engine.
Updated emissions and electronics requiring strict engine code matching for replacements.
Launch of the top-tier "Platinum" trim level.
knownFor: The 3.6L Pentastar offering much better refinement and fuel economy than its predecessor.
New failure patterns emerging, specifically oil cooler leaks leading to head gasket issues.
Highly complex electronics making DIY engine swaps nearly impossible, driving "supply & fit" demand.
years.cta: Find a rebuilt 2011-2016 Chrysler 300 / 300C engine today
years.ticker: Chrysler 300C 3.6 Pentastar oil cooler leak • Chrysler 300C 2012+ engine compatibility

engineYears.closing: Not sure which year your Chrysler 300 / 300C was built? Enter your registration number and we'll identify the exact engine, model year, and compatible replacement options instantly - all backed by a minimum 12‑month warranty from UK Chrysler 300 / 300C specialists.

faq.h2: Chrysler 300 / 300C Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Chrysler 300 / 300C engine replacement cost?
items.answer: A rebuilt Chrysler 300 / 300C engine typically costs between £1,400 and £3,200 for supply only, depending on the engine code. Adding professional supply and fit installation generally adds £600 to £1,200 to the total bill.
keyPoints: 3.0L CRD (EXL) rebuilt units average £1,400 to £2,600.
5.7L HEMI (EZB) rebuilt units average £1,800 to £3,200.
Supply and fit service includes labour, fluids, and a unified warranty.
Prices exclude ancillary parts like new DPFs or EGR valves if required.
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Chrysler 300 / 300C engine prices today

items.question: What is the most common engine problem in the Chrysler 300 / 300C?
items.answer: The most frequent catastrophic failures are timing chain stretch in the 3.0L CRD (EXL) diesel and hydraulic lifter tick leading to camshaft wear in the 5.7L HEMI (EZB) petrol. Both issues are well-documented and directly addressed in our rebuilt units.
keyPoints: EXL timing chains often fail between 80,000 and 100,000 miles.
EZB lifter tick is caused by oiling issues and collapsed lifters.
Ignoring early rattling or tapping noises guarantees expensive internal damage.
Rebuilt engines feature upgraded chains or valvetrain components to prevent recurrence.
items.cta: Get a quote for a fixed 3.0 CRD or 5.7 HEMI replacement

items.question: How long does a Chrysler 300 / 300C engine replacement take?
items.answer: The typical turnaround time for a Chrysler 300 / 300C engine replacement is 3 to 5 working days from the point of order confirmation. This includes sourcing the unit, palletised delivery, and professional garage installation.
keyPoints: Sourcing and dispatching the rebuilt engine takes 1-2 working days.
Professional supply and fit installation requires 1-2 days in the garage.
Complex variants like the SRT8 may require slight additional sourcing time.
We coordinate directly with the garage to minimise your vehicle's downtime.
items.cta: Arrange a fast supply and fit for your Chrysler 300 / 300C

items.question: What warranty comes with a reconditioned Chrysler 300 / 300C engine?
items.answer: Every reconditioned or rebuilt engine supplied through our network includes a minimum 12-month, unlimited mileage warranty as standard. This covers both parts and labour, providing complete peace of mind.
keyPoints: The warranty is valid across the entire UK through our approved garage network.
It specifically covers internal mechanical failures of the supplied unit.
Regular servicing with the correct oil specifications must be maintained.
Always confirm the specific warranty documentation with your chosen supplier before fitting.
items.cta: Find a reconditioned Chrysler 300 / 300C engine with 12-month warranty

items.question: Can I upgrade my Chrysler 300 / 300C from a V6 to a HEMI V8?
items.answer: While physically possible, swapping a V6 for a 5.7L HEMI in a Chrysler 300 / 300C is highly complex and rarely cost-effective due to extensive ECU, wiring harness, and mounting modifications required.
keyPoints: The V6 and V8 use entirely different engine management systems.
Custom wiring harnesses and ECU reprogramming are mandatory.
Mounting points and transmission bell housings may differ significantly.
We recommend replacing like-for-like to ensure reliability and legal compliance.

items.question: How do I find my exact Chrysler 300 / 300C engine code?
items.answer: You can find your engine code in three places: on your V5C logbook (Section D.2), on the manufacturer's VIN plate under the bonnet, or stamped directly onto the engine block near the cylinder head.
keyPoints: The V5C registration document is the fastest and most reliable method.
Common codes are EXL (3.0 Diesel), EZB (5.7 Petrol), and ERB (3.6 Petrol).
Providing the exact code guarantees 100% compatibility for your replacement.
Our registration lookup tool can also identify this automatically for you.
items.cta: Use our free instant Chrysler 300 / 300C engine code checker

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in an older Chrysler 300 / 300C?
items.answer: Yes, replacing the engine is often significantly more cost-effective than purchasing a new vehicle, especially given the 300 / 300C's enduring comfort, road presence, and the high cost of modern depreciation.
keyPoints: A rebuilt engine restores the car to better-than-used reliability.
It costs a fraction of the monthly repayments on a new executive car.
The vehicle retains its core value, interior luxury, and driving dynamics.
Supply and fit options protect you from unexpected mechanic markups.
items.cta: See if a Chrysler 300 / 300C engine replacement is worth it for you

items.question: Do you offer nationwide supply and fit for Chrysler 300 / 300C engines?
items.answer: Yes, we offer a comprehensive, nationwide supply and fit service, connecting you with vetted, specialist garages across the UK who are experienced in handling heavy V6 and V8 engine installations.
keyPoints: We handle the logistics, delivering the engine directly to your chosen garage.
All partner garages are reviewed and rated by other Chrysler owners.
You receive a single, unified warranty covering both the engine and the labour.
No need to source a separate mechanic or arrange complex engine transportation.
items.cta: Book a nationwide supply and fit for your Chrysler 300 / 300C

items.question: What is the difference between rebuilt and reconditioned 300 / 300C engines?
items.answer: While often used interchangeably, "reconditioned" typically implies a more thorough, ground-up restoration to OEM specifications, whereas "rebuilt" may focus on replacing only the specific failed components and associated wear items.
keyPoints: Reconditioned engines are completely stripped, cleaned, and machined.
Rebuilt engines address the root cause of the specific failure (e.g., lifters).
Both options from our network undergo rigorous bench testing before dispatch.
Both come with the same robust 12-month, unlimited mileage warranty.
items.cta: Compare rebuilt vs reconditioned Chrysler 300 / 300C engine prices

items.question: Does the replacement engine come with an ECU?
items.answer: No, replacement engines are supplied as long or short blocks and do not include the Engine Control Unit (ECU). Your vehicle's existing ECU is retained, reinstalled, and cleared of fault codes by the fitting garage.
keyPoints: The ECU is coded to your vehicle's chassis and immobiliser system.
Swapping the ECU would require complex, expensive dealer-level reprogramming.
Specialist garages will perform an ECU reset to integrate the new engine seamlessly.
Ensure your garage has the diagnostic tools required for the ECU adaptation.
items.cta: Get quotes for Chrysler 300 / 300C engine supply only
